medications.indication_rationale: Prompt oral antiviral to halt viral replication in a periocular HSV outbreak; start within the early efficacy window.
medications.dose_route_frequency: episodic high-dose regimen per source note (e.g., 2 g then 2 g 12 h later) — confirm per prescriber/renal function
contraindications_in_this_case: Adjust dose in renal impairment; ensure hydration

medications.drug_name: topical corticosteroid to the eye/steroid drops (self-directed)
medications.indication_rationale: Topical ocular steroids can worsen herpetic keratitis and must not be self-started; any eye treatment is for ophthalmology.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Strict hand hygiene — do not touch/pop the blisters or the eye, avoid makeup and contact lenses, and don't share towels.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Prevents autoinoculation of the virus into the eye and transmission to others.

patient_education_points.point: This is a cold-sore (herpes) outbreak triggered by stress and sun. Because it's close to your eye, you're starting antiviral tablets now and need your eye checked in person tomorrow.
Wash your hands carefully and don't touch your eye or the blisters — the virus can be carried to the eye on your fingers. No makeup or contact lenses on that side.
If your eye becomes red or painful, sensitive to light, feels gritty, or your vision blurs — go to an eye emergency department immediately.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Eye examination (slit-lamp/fluorescein), lesion progression, and antiviral response; ophthalmology if any ocular signs.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Red/painful eye, photophobia, gritty feeling, or blurred vision — attend an eye emergency immediately.

must_include_items: Diagnose periocular cutaneous HSV and take the ocular risk seriously
Start oral antivirals promptly (early efficacy window)
Strict hand hygiene to prevent ocular autoinoculation; no self-started ocular steroids
Urgent next-day in-person/ophthalmic review AND a clear ocular safety-net (cannot exclude keratitis remotely)
acceptable_alternatives: Direct ophthalmology review if readily available
errors_of_commission: Falsely reassuring the eye is fine without an exam
Dismissing the outbreak as a simple cold sore
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires prompt antivirals PLUS an ocular safety-net and urgent in-person eye review, without false reassurance that the eye is unaffected.
